phaseName: Build Flask service and run it in the background
goal: Implement /app/app.py serving POST /sentiment per the API schema, launch it on 0.0.0.0:5000 as a detached background process, and verify all success criteria.
summary: Phase 2 produced and shipped a working Flask sentiment service. /app/app.py (72 lines) loads the local DistilBERT SST-2 model at import time under TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1, exposes POST /sentiment per the documented contract, and binds to 0.0.0.0:5000 with debug=False and use_reloader=False. The service was launched as PID 762 under setsid+nohup, logging to /app/app.log. End-to-end verification confirmed: positive → 200 positive (0.999882), negative → 200 negative (0.999646), {} / non-string / whitespace → 400 with {"error":...}. Port 5000 confirmed LISTEN on 0.0.0.0 via /proc/net/tcp. Container lacked curl/ss/ps so verification used python3+urllib+/proc only — no installs were added.
stepSummaries:
1. Write /app/app.py: load the local model once at startup, expose POST /sentiment that accepts {"text": ...}, returns {sentiment, confidence:{positive,negative}}, and returns 400 {"error": ...} on missing/empty/non-string text or invalid JSON. Bind to 0.0.0.0:5000. [verified]
  2. Launch /app/app.py as a detached background process (nohup ... & or setsid) logging to /app/app.log, then wait for the port to be listening. [verified]
  3. Verify the API end-to-end: positive text returns sentiment=positive with positive>negative; negative text returns sentiment=negative with negative>positive; missing/invalid text returns HTTP 400 with {"error": ...}. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: All three steps used real verification. Step 1 used an AST parse plus an import smoke that loaded the actual model and reported the route table and id2label. Step 2 used /proc/net/tcp + /proc/<pid>/cmdline (the container's available substitute for ss/pgrep) plus a urllib smoke round-trip. Step 3 ran five real HTTP requests against the live service with assertions on status codes and JSON bodies. No proxy-only steps.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Step 1: AST ok + import printed `routes: ['/sentiment', '/sentiment']` + `NEG_ID=0 POS_ID=1`. Step 2: LISTEN 00000000:1388 + pid 762 cmdline = `python3 -u /app/app.py`. Step 3: /tmp/verify_api.py printed `ALL_PASS` exit 0 across 5 cases.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal is "implement the Flask app, launch it detached, and verify all success criteria." The /app/app.py source exists, the process is detached (setsid+nohup PID 762, log to /app/app.log), port 5000 is bound on 0.0.0.0, and the API contract is satisfied end-to-end (positive/negative/error responses all match schema). Every success criterion in the plan has at least one piece of evidence in this report.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Criteria 2-5 are directly observed; criteria 6-7 (detached + listening on 0.0.0.0) are confirmed via /proc.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred. All three planned steps executed and verified. The /tmp/verify_api.py and /tmp/dl_model.py helpers are throwaway test files under /tmp, not artifacts of the shipped service — they don't pollute /app.
gateVerdicts.evidence: Phase plan had 3 steps; all completed with terminal verdicts; no skipped or failed items.
